data.description:
Extract a shared, parameterized base for OSCER's "OSCER-shipped defaults + deployment override + boot validation" config loaders, and refactor the existing loaders onto it.

## Origin
Surfaced in the review of Jeff Dettmann's "Data Source Hierarchy" tech spec (2026-07-01). The spec proposes three MORE config loaders modeled on the existing Exemption config pattern (exception-check config, exclusion-priority config, data-source registry). Combined with the two that already exist, that would be FIVE hand-rolled loaders with the same load/merge/validate discipline — past rule-of-three.

Jeff greenlit this in Slack (2026-07-01): "Sounds like a great idea! You can run with it if you don't have anything more pressing." So this is spec-owner-blessed and owned by Mike. Conditional priority (only if nothing more pressing; currently competes with the application-form refactor C1).

## Current state (as of 2026-07-01)
- `app/services/exemption_types_loader.rb` — DEFAULTS + `config/custom/exemption_types.yml` deep-merge + boot validation; wired in `config/initializers/exemption_types.rb`. Documented in CUSTOMIZATION.md.
- `app/services/feature_flags_loader.rb` — same discipline, but additive-only semantics (its own comment says it "mirrors ExemptionTypesLoader's load/validate discipline"; cannot disable built-ins; raises on collision).

So the seam to generalize already exists: the two loaders differ mainly in MERGE STRATEGY (deep-merge vs additive-only).

## Scope
- Design a base (parameterized by: DEFAULTS source, merge strategy, per-entry validation) that both existing loaders express as thin configs.
- Merge strategies to support: deep-merge (exemption types), additive-only (feature flags), and — for the spec's future needs — ordered-list/priority (exclusion priority) and additive-with-metadata (data-source registry).
- Refactor `ExemptionTypesLoader` and `FeatureFlagsLoader` onto the base with no behavior change (their existing specs are the regression net).
- Preserve ALL current boot-validation and ConfigurationError behavior.

## Relationship to the Data Source Hierarchy work
This is a foundation/prerequisite for the three config loaders the spec proposes. Doing it first means those three become thin configs of the base rather than three more hand-rolled copies. Not a blocker for the spec's other work items, but highest-leverage if done before them.

## References
- Review notes (vault): Tech Specs/OSCER/data-source-hierarchy-review.md
- Spec: Data Source Hierarchy (Jeff Dettmann), https://docs.google.com/document/d/1NeAEnIwRnBgtwWt_7tonPDdIZYh_KryhvQhHxXGPs6Q/edit
- Existing loaders: app/services/exemption_types_loader.rb, app/services/feature_flags_loader.rb
- Deployment-override docs: CUSTOMIZATION.md
data.title: Extract shared config-registry base class (default + deployment-override loaders)
